976a4f4d.code:
def descriptor(rA, rB, rX, nA, nB, nX):
    return (abs(nA * rA - nX * rX) + abs(nB * rB - nX * rX)) / (rA + rB + rX)
976a4f4d.description: This descriptor measures the mismatch between the ionic radius scaled by oxidation state (reflecting effective charge density) for A and X, as well as B and X, capturing how well the ionic sizes and charges complement each other to stabilize the structure. Smaller values suggest better charge-size compatibility, which promotes stability beyond simple size ratios.

7f03d563.code:
def descriptor(rA, rB, rX, nA, nB, nX):
    return (abs(nA) * rA - abs(nX) * rX) / (abs(nB) * rB + 1)
7f03d563.description: This descriptor combines ionic radii and oxidation states by emphasizing the charge coupling between A and X, which influences electrostatic attraction and bonding strength. The numerator captures how the A cation's size, weighted by its charge, compares to the X anion's size weighted by its charge, reflecting the electrostatic fit. The denominator accounts for the B cation's size and charge, balancing the overall electrostatic environment. This formulation aims to incorporate charge effects beyond simple size ratios, addressing variations in bonding strength and electrostatic stability that influence perovskite formation.
7f03d563.formula: \frac{\left| n_A \right| r_A - \left| n_X \right| r_X}{\left| n_B \right| r_B + 1}

816c9e9f.code:
def descriptor(rA, rB, rX, nA, nB, nX):
    return (abs(nA) * rA * nA + abs(nX) * rX * nX) / (abs(nB) * rB + 1)
816c9e9f.description: This descriptor incorporates the effect of oxidation states on electrostatic attraction and bonding strength, emphasizing the charge coupling between ions. By multiplying ionic radii by their respective oxidation states, it accounts for the influence of charge magnitude on the ion's effective size and bonding environment, beyond simple size ratios. The numerator captures the electrostatic contribution from A and X ions, while the denominator normalizes by B's size and charge, reflecting the overall electrostatic stability. This formulation aims to address the shortcomings of purely size-based descriptors by including charge effects, which are critical for stability especially across different anion types.

3022a9f5.code:
def descriptor(rA, rB, rX, nA, nB, nX):
    return (abs(nA) * rA - abs(nX) * rX) / (abs(nA) + abs(nX))
3022a9f5.description: This descriptor combines ionic radii weighted by their absolute oxidation states to account for charge-dependent bonding effects. It captures the relative size and charge compatibility between the A-site cation and the X-site anion, addressing the influence of electrostatic attraction and bonding preferences beyond simple size ratios. By emphasizing the charge-scaled size difference between A and X, it better discriminates stable perovskite formation, especially across different anion types with variable electronegativities.
